title: IBM-Aberdeen Meeting: Building Value Chain Leaders
author: Jamie Bedard, Peter Kastner, Tim Minahan, Chris Jones, Tom Ryan, John Farrell (Aberdeen Group)
abstract: Aberdeen Group consulting pitch deck to IBM proposing a research, benchmark, and workshop alignment program focused on Supply Chain Management (SCM), Procurement, and Strategic Consulting. Identifies IBM's On Demand validation, market visibility, and intellectual capital needs; proposes a three-phase approach combining Aberdeen Online Conference Series (AOCS), benchmark studies, installed base assessments, and joint workshops to grow IBM's top-line revenue and document business-value impact. Flag: Aberdeen-internal / client-confidential.
importance_rationale: Formative Aberdeen-IBM working deck establishing the research-agenda-sponsorship consulting model that became Aberdeen's primary revenue engine in the mid-2000s. Illustrates how Aberdeen positioned itself as trusted advisor to G5000 solution providers.
relevance_rationale: Research-sponsorship and benchmark-led consulting models remain relevant for analyst firms and B2B marketing; IBM On Demand branding is historical but the lead-generation mechanics are enduring.
prescience_rationale: Primarily an engagement proposal rather than predictive analysis; limited forward-looking claims beyond proposed Q1-Q3 research agenda.
